reasoning_content:
Wait, I think I misunderstood the line marker format. Let me look at the preprocessed file again:

```
# 1 "/workspace/YatCC/test/cases/functional-0/000_main.sysu.c"
# 1 "<built-in>" 1
# 1 "<built-in>" 3
# 389 "<built-in>" 3
# 1 "<command line>" 1
# 1 "<built-in>" 2
# 1 "/workspace/YatCC/test/cases/functional-0/000_main.sysu.c" 2
int main(){
    return 3;
}
```

The format is: `# linenum filename flags`

So `# 1 "/workspace/YatCC/test/cases/functional-0/000_main.sysu.c"` means the next line is line 1 of that file.
And `# 1 "/workspace/YatCC/test/cases/functional-0/000_main.sysu.c" 2` means the next line is line 1 of that file, and it's entering from a system header (flag 2).

So the line marker tells us what line number the NEXT line corresponds to in the source file.

Let me fix this properly.

tool_call.name: file_editor
